perturbation.original_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information” shall mean all information provided by Disclosing Party with respect to the Invention regardless of whether it is written, oral, audio tapes, video tapes, computer disc s, machines, prototypes, designs, specifications, articles of manufacture, drawings, huma n or machine readable documents.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 “Confidential Information” shall mean some information provided by Disclosing Party with respect to the Invention.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the original definition of “Confidential Information” and omitting the rest of the information, the definition becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction as later clauses that require keeping the Confidential Information secure, are now ambiguous, and therefore harder to enforce. This particularly affects Section 3 which discusses the return of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly to include 'all information'. The changed text narrows this definition to 'some information', creating ambiguity about what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts Section 3, which requires the return of 'all Confidential Information'. Because the changed text makes it unclear exactly what information falls under the umbrella of "Confidential Information", the Receiving Party's obligation in Section 3 to return "all Confidential Information" becomes unenforceable as the definition of said information is now vague. This constitutes a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) Receive and maintain the Confidential Information in confidence; (b) Examine the Confidential Information at its own expense; (c) Not reproduce the Confidential Information or any part thereof without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (d) Not, directly or indirectly , make known, divulge, publish or communicate the Confidential Information to any person, firm or corporation without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; (e) Limit the internal dissemination of the Confidential Information and the internal disclosure of the Confid ential Information received from the Disclosing Party to those officers and employees, if any, of the Receiving Party who have a need to know and an obligation to protect it; (f) Not use or utilize the Confidential Information without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 3 (g) Not use the Confidential Information or any part thereof as a basis for the design or creation of any method, system, apparatus or device similar to any method, system, apparatus or device embodied in the Confidential Information unless expressly authorized in writing by Disclosing Party; a n d (h) Utilize the best efforts possible to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information from loss, theft, destruction, or the like.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. USE OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Receiving Party agrees to: (a) Receive and maintain the Confidential Information; (b) Examine the Confidential Information at its own expense; (c) Not reproduce the Confidential Information or any part thereof; (d) Not, directly or indirectly , make known the Confidential Information to any person; (e) Limit the internal dissemination of the Confidential Information received from the Disclosing Party to those officers and employees of the Receiving Party; (f) Not use the Confidential Information without the express written consent of Disclosing Party; Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 3 (g) Not use the Confidential Information as a basis for the design of any method similar to any method embodied in the Confidential Information; a n d (h) Utilize efforts to protect the Confidential Information from loss.
perturbation.explanation: Removing key phrases from Section 2 significantly weakens the protection of Confidential Information. For instance, omitting "in confidence" from (a), "without the express written consent of Disclosing Party" from (c) and (d), "if any, who have a need to know and an obligation to protect it" from (e), "any part thereof" from (g) and "best efforts possible" from (h). This creates ambiguity about the required level of care, potentially contradicting the overall intent of the NDA, and weakens Disclosing Party's rights in Section 5 regarding governing law and interpretation of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. GOVERNING LAW This agreement and all questions relating to its validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement (including, without limitation, provisions concerning limitations of actions), shall be governed by and cons trued in accordance with the laws of the State Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 4 of Oregon, notwithstanding any conflict-of -laws doctrines of such state or other jurisdiction to the contrary, and without th e aid of any canon, custom or rule of law requiring construction against the draftsman.
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits key phrases that weaken the protection of confidential information, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This directly contradicts Section 5, which relies on a strong and enforceable agreement to be governed by Oregon law. The removal of phrases like 'in confidence', 'without the express written consent of Disclosing Party', and 'best efforts possible' introduces ambiguity and weakens the Disclosing Party's rights, impacting the 'interpretation, performance and enforcement' of the agreement outlined in Section 5. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION All information provided by the Disclosing Party shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party agrees to return all Confidential Information to Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whet her or not to work with the Disclosing Party, Receiving Party shall return all information to the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Receiving Party agrees to return Confidential Information to Disclosing Party upon request. Receiving Party shall return information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the condition where the receiving party agrees to return all Confidential Information to the Disclosing Party within 15 days of written demand by Disclosing Party. When the Receiving Party has finished reviewing the information provided by the Disclosing Party and has made a decision as to whether or not to work with the Disclosing Party without retaining any copies, creates an in-text contradiction as the original text that refers to all information that belongs to the Disclosing Party, does not have the return by the Receiving Party in a detailed way.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Receiving Party agrees to return Confidential Information to Disclosing Party upon request. Receiving Party shall return information to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text details specific conditions for returning confidential information, including a 15-day window after written demand and a requirement to return information after a decision is made, without retaining copies. The changed text removes these specifics, stating only that the Receiving Party agrees to return Confidential Information upon request. This omission creates a contradiction as the original, more detailed conditions are no longer present or compatible with the changed text, as referenced by the contradiction text, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the return of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 5. GOVERNING LAW This agreement and all questions relating to its validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement (including, without limitation, provisions concerning limitations of actions), shall be governed by and cons trued in accordance with the laws of the State Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 4 of Oregon, notwithstanding any conflict-of -laws doctrines of such state or other jurisdiction to the contrary, and without th e aid of any canon, custom or rule of law requiring construction against the draftsman.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. GOVERNING LAW This agreement and all questions relating to its interpretation, performance and enforcement shall be governed by and cons trued in accordance with the laws of the State Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 4 of Oregon.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the part of the original sentence that mentions: 'This agreement and all questions relating to its validity (including, without limitation, provisions concerning limitations of actions), notwithstanding any conflict-of -laws doctrines of such state or other jurisdiction to the contrary, and without th e aid of any canon, custom or rule of law requiring construction against the draftsman.', creates ambiguity and possible contradictions because questions relating to its validity, and concerning limitations of actions, conflict-of -laws doctrines of such state or other jurisdiction, and aid of any canon, custom or rule of law requiring construction against the draftsman are not accounted for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. GOVERNING LAW This agreement and all questions relating to its interpretation, performance and enforcement shall be governed by and cons trued in accordance with the laws of the State Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 4 of Oregon.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly addresses the agreement's validity, limitations of actions, conflict-of-laws doctrines, and construction rules. The changed text omits these considerations. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text which also doesn't account for validity, limitations of actions, conflict-of-laws doctrines, and construction rules. The omission in the changed text directly contradicts the original intent by neglecting key aspects, leading to potential ambiguity and conflict with how the agreement is interpreted and enforced, which creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.
